texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) Graphical User Interface (GUI) is a Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture (VistA) computer application. CPRS enables you to enter, review, and continuously update information connected with a patient. With CPRS, you can order lab tests, medications, diets, radiology tests and procedures, record a patient’s allergies or adverse reactions to medications, request and track consults, enter progress notes, diagnoses, and treatments for each encounter, and enter discharge summaries. In addition, CPRS supports clinical decision-making and enables you to review and analyze patient data.
texts.text: The Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) Graphical User Interface (GUI) is a Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture (VistA) computer application. CPRS enables you to enter, review, and continuously update information connected with a patient. With CPRS, you can order lab tests, medications, diets, radiology tests and procedures, record a patient’s allergies or adverse reactions to medications, request and track consults, enter progress notes, diagnoses, and treatments for each encounter, and enter discharge summaries. In addition, CPRS supports clinical decision-making and enables you to review and analyze patient data.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of this guide is to help sites prepare for installation of CPRS v.29 and its associated patches. Changes in CPRS v.29 are significant in that they affect the ability of providers to order medications, specifically outpatient controlled substances. New DEA regulations affect the ability to electronically prescribe controlled substances, even though they are only “transmitted” to the Outpatient Pharmacy at the VA Medical Center. The regulations require each provider to be individually set up with specific information, such as the number that gives them the authority to prescribe controlled substances (DEA or VA number, and, if necessary, Detox/Maintenance number, including an expiration date), a subject alternative name , and any schedules for which they can or cannot prescribe. In addition, there must be a second “granting” mechanism, controlled by an office different from the office assigning the DEA number and schedules. This set up should be done PRIOR to the CPRS v.29 installation into your production account.
texts.text: The purpose of this guide is to help sites prepare for installation of CPRS v.29 and its associated patches. Changes in CPRS v.29 are significant in that they affect the ability of providers to order medications, specifically outpatient controlled substances. New DEA regulations affect the ability to electronically prescribe controlled substances, even though they are only “transmitted” to the Outpatient Pharmacy at the VA Medical Center. The regulations require each provider to be individually set up with specific information, such as the number that gives them the authority to prescribe controlled substances (DEA or VA number, and, if necessary, Detox/Maintenance number, including an expiration date), a subject alternative name , and any schedules for which they can or cannot prescribe. In addition, there must be a second “granting” mechanism, controlled by an office different from the office assigning the DEA number and schedules. This set up should be done PRIOR to the CPRS v.29 installation into your production account.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition, DEA ePrescribing requires that Public Key Infrastucture (PKI) be used to maintain the security of controlled substances orders. To do this, several components must be installed or configured:
texts.text: In addition, DEA ePrescribing requires that Public Key Infrastucture (PKI) be used to maintain the security of controlled substances orders. To do this, several components must be installed or configured:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PKISERVER.EXE, which runs as a service on a Windows Server (this helps verify the order information when it was placed is the same as it is when the pharmacy finishes the prescription)
texts.text: PKISERVER.EXE, which runs as a service on a Windows Server (this helps verify the order information when it was placed is the same as it is when the pharmacy finishes the prescription)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PKIVerifyServerSetup.EXE, which installs the PKISERVER.EXE as a service on the Windows Server.
texts.text: PKIVerifyServerSetup.EXE, which installs the PKISERVER.EXE as a service on the Windows Server.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ePCSDataEntryforPrescriber.exe, which enables users to enter the credential information for the prescriber (the appropriate number and expiration date) and the schedules that the prescriber has authority to write controlled substance orders for.
texts.text: ePCSDataEntryforPrescriber.exe, which enables users to enter the credential information for the prescriber (the appropriate number and expiration date) and the schedules that the prescriber has authority to write controlled substance orders for.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OE/RR EPCS PARAMETERS file (#100.7), which provides the second granting authority for controlled substance prescriptions. This is referred to as a logical access control.
texts.text: OE/RR EPCS PARAMETERS file (#100.7), which provides the second granting authority for controlled substance prescriptions. This is referred to as a logical access control.

texts.orig: The PKI Verify Service is used during the Pharmacy process of completing controlled substance orders entered in CPRS. It is used to validate that the contents of the prescription have not been modified. Sites will need to install the PKI Verify Service on a minimum of one and maximum of three Windows servers.
texts.text: The PKI Verify Service is used during the Pharmacy process of completing controlled substance orders entered in CPRS. It is used to validate that the contents of the prescription have not been modified. Sites will need to install the PKI Verify Service on a minimum of one and maximum of three Windows servers.

texts.text: 5.3 Install PKI Verify Service on One to Three Windows Servers

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following instructions will help you install the PKI Verify Service and run it on at least one, but as many as three Windows servers. This service is used during the Pharmacy finishing process to verify that the provider PKI certificate is still valid.
texts.text: The following instructions will help you install the PKI Verify Service and run it on at least one, but as many as three Windows servers. This service is used during the Pharmacy finishing process to verify that the provider PKI certificate is still valid.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You will install the PKI Verify Service on the same servers you used in step 5.1 (you must have at least one, but can have up to three).
texts.text: You will install the PKI Verify Service on the same servers you used in step 5.1 (you must have at least one, but can have up to three).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Identify the person who will install the PKI Verify Service. (See requirements above). This is probably the same person who accomplished section 5.1 above.
texts.text: Identify the person who will install the PKI Verify Service. (See requirements above). This is probably the same person who accomplished section 5.1 above.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Open CPRS29_DEA_KERNEL.ZIP file by double-clicking it or selecting it and pressing <Enter>.
texts.text: Open CPRS29_DEA_KERNEL.ZIP file by double-clicking it or selecting it and pressing <Enter>.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Copy the PKIVERIFYSERVERSETUP.EXE to the Windows Server on which you want  PKI Verify Service to run.
texts.text: Copy the PKIVERIFYSERVERSETUP.EXE to the Windows Server on which you want  PKI Verify Service to run.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Run PKIVERIFYSERVERSETUP.EXE by right-clicking on it and selecting Run as Administrator.
texts.text: Run PKIVERIFYSERVERSETUP.EXE by right-clicking on it and selecting Run as Administrator.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PKIVERIFYSERVERSETUP.EXE will install PKISERVER.EXE in the C:\Windows directory and start it as an automatic service. If the service has been previously installed, it will stop the service, remove the service, and then replace the earlier version of PKIServer.exe before setting up and starting the service again.
texts.text: PKIVERIFYSERVERSETUP.EXE will install PKISERVER.EXE in the C:\Windows directory and start it as an automatic service. If the service has been previously installed, it will stop the service, remove the service, and then replace the earlier version of PKIServer.exe before setting up and starting the service again.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If you look at services on the Windows server, PKISERVER.EXE will appear as PKI_Verify_Service.
texts.text: If you look at services on the Windows server, PKISERVER.EXE will appear as PKI_Verify_Service.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Record the IP address of the Windows Server.
texts.text: Record the IP address of the Windows Server.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Server IP addresses will be need in the next section to set up the PKI parameters.
texts.text: The Server IP addresses will be need in the next section to set up the PKI parameters.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (Optional) Repeat steps 3 - 5 for up to two more servers.
texts.text: (Optional) Repeat steps 3 - 5 for up to two more servers.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Below is an example of the dialogs that the user installing PKIServer.exe will see:
texts.text: Below is an example of the dialogs that the user installing PKIServer.exe will see:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: At this time, another window will open that displays the command line items as they execute.
texts.text: At this time, another window will open that displays the command line items as they execute.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 6 Set Up a Job to Periodically Restart the PKI Verify Service (temporary mitigation strategy)
texts.text: 6 Set Up a Job to Periodically Restart the PKI Verify Service (temporary mitigation strategy)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: During testing, two sites experienced issues with the PKI Verify Service communication stopping after the initial communication from VistA was successful. In both cases, it was necessary to restart the service to clear the issue. Currently, this is believed to be an issue with communicating with the national revocation server.
texts.text: During testing, two sites experienced issues with the PKI Verify Service communication stopping after the initial communication from VistA was successful. In both cases, it was necessary to restart the service to clear the issue. Currently, this is believed to be an issue with communicating with the national revocation server.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It was determined that periodically restarting the PKI Verify Service is an appropriate, although temporary, mitigation strategy.
texts.text: It was determined that periodically restarting the PKI Verify Service is an appropriate, although temporary, mitigation strategy.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Set up a Windows job to restart the service periodically – preferably 3 times per week.
texts.text: Set up a Windows job to restart the service periodically – preferably 3 times per week.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Someone who can edit Kernel parameters needs to perform this step.
texts.text: Someone who can edit Kernel parameters needs to perform this step.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In VistA, edit the Kernel System Parameters file (#8989.3) by using the following options:
texts.text: In VistA, edit the Kernel System Parameters file (#8989.3) by using the following options:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Then choose either of the following:
texts.text: Then choose either of the following:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Enter the IP addresses separated by commas for the servers where PKISERVER.EXE is installed. You must have at least one IP address because PKISERVER.EXE must be installed on at least one server. You can install it on up to two additional servers as well. These entries will be stored in the PKI Server field (#53.1).
texts.text: Enter the IP addresses separated by commas for the servers where PKISERVER.EXE is installed. You must have at least one IP address because PKISERVER.EXE must be installed on at least one server. You can install it on up to two additional servers as well. These entries will be stored in the PKI Server field (#53.1).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: From the CPRS29_DEA_KERNEL.ZIP file, you can distribute the ePCSDataEntryforPrescriber.exe. To help sites with the set up for DEA ePrescribing, the Kernel developers created this GUI application. It will enable users to enter the appropriate information for individual providers who write controlled substance medication orders.
texts.text: From the CPRS29_DEA_KERNEL.ZIP file, you can distribute the ePCSDataEntryforPrescriber.exe. To help sites with the set up for DEA ePrescribing, the Kernel developers created this GUI application. It will enable users to enter the appropriate information for individual providers who write controlled substance medication orders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Who needs this executable? The office or group of people who will be responsible for the set up of items related to the provider’s credentials regarding controlled substances prescribing—DEA number, VA number, Detox/Maintenance number, and schedules allowed.
texts.text: Who needs this executable? The office or group of people who will be responsible for the set up of items related to the provider’s credentials regarding controlled substances prescribing—DEA number, VA number, Detox/Maintenance number, and schedules allowed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To enter data in the ePCS Data Entry for Prescriber application, thporte user must have the XUEPCSEDIT key.
texts.text: To enter data in the ePCS Data Entry for Prescriber application, thporte user must have the XUEPCSEDIT key.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A user cannot have both the XUEPCSEDIT key and the ORES key. The two keys are exclusive to one another so that the person prescribing is not also the person controlling authorization for prescribing controlled substances.
texts.text: A user cannot have both the XUEPCSEDIT key and the ORES key. The two keys are exclusive to one another so that the person prescribing is not also the person controlling authorization for prescribing controlled substances.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Use the following steps to distribute:
texts.text: Use the following steps to distribute:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If it is not already open, locate and open the CPRS29_DEA_KERNEL.ZIP file by double-clicking it or highlighting it and pressing <Enter>.
texts.text: If it is not already open, locate and open the CPRS29_DEA_KERNEL.ZIP file by double-clicking it or highlighting it and pressing <Enter>.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Distribute or install ePCSDataEntryforPresciber.exe following your normal processes.
texts.text: Distribute or install ePCSDataEntryforPresciber.exe following your normal processes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Veterans Administration (VA) has made changes to implement the Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) requirements for ordering controlled substances. The DEA now requires two-factor authentication and acknowledgement that the provider accepts what signing for a controlled substance means. This message displays in the Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) signature dialogs when a provider signs controlled substances orders as outpatient prescriptions.
texts.text: The Veterans Administration (VA) has made changes to implement the Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) requirements for ordering controlled substances. The DEA now requires two-factor authentication and acknowledgement that the provider accepts what signing for a controlled substance means. This message displays in the Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) signature dialogs when a provider signs controlled substances orders as outpatient prescriptions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For controlled substance orders on an outpatient prescription, the process now includes two-factor authentication. When providers begin to sign an order for a controlled substance, they must first select which outpatient controlled substance orders they will sign. Then, the provider will enter their electronic signature, insert their Personal Identification Verification (PIV) card, and enter the Personal Identification Number (PIN) for the PIV card. The software then checks to ensure that the provider has a valid DEA number and PKI certificate. To check the certificate, the software requires specific information for each user.
texts.text: For controlled substance orders on an outpatient prescription, the process now includes two-factor authentication. When providers begin to sign an order for a controlled substance, they must first select which outpatient controlled substance orders they will sign. Then, the provider will enter their electronic signature, insert their Personal Identification Verification (PIV) card, and enter the Personal Identification Number (PIN) for the PIV card. The software then checks to ensure that the provider has a valid DEA number and PKI certificate. To check the certificate, the software requires specific information for each user.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 9.2 A New Application to Enter DEA Information
texts.text: 9.2 A New Application to Enter DEA Information

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To enter the required information for ordering and signing controlled substance orders, VA developers created the new Data Entry for e-Prescribing Controlled Substances (ePCS) GUI application. The application enables users to select prescribers one at a time and enter the following information for the selected provider:
texts.text: To enter the required information for ordering and signing controlled substance orders, VA developers created the new Data Entry for e-Prescribing Controlled Substances (ePCS) GUI application. The application enables users to select prescribers one at a time and enter the following information for the selected provider:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: User’s Subject Alternative Name (which is, generally, their VA Outlook  address, if they have one)
texts.text: User’s Subject Alternative Name (which is, generally, their VA Outlook  address, if they have one)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For which Drug Schedules the Provider may write orders
texts.text: For which Drug Schedules the Provider may write orders

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This is the Data Entry for e-Prescribing Controlled Substances dialog. Here it is shown after the user has connected to the account, but before selecting a prescriber whose information needs to be edited.
texts.text: This is the Data Entry for e-Prescribing Controlled Substances dialog. Here it is shown after the user has connected to the account, but before selecting a prescriber whose information needs to be edited.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If a provider already has some of this information, it will display when the user selects the provider’s name. For example, if they are already authorized to write medication orders, the check box will be checked. If the user already has a DEA number, the DEA number will display and so forth.
texts.text: If a provider already has some of this information, it will display when the user selects the provider’s name. For example, if they are already authorized to write medication orders, the check box will be checked. If the user already has a DEA number, the DEA number will display and so forth.

texts.orig: 9.3 Logging In to the Data Entry for ePrescribing Controlled Substances Application
texts.text: 9.3 Logging In to the Data Entry for ePrescribing Controlled Substances Application

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To log in to the Data Entry for ePrescribing Controlled Substances GUI application, a user holding the XUEPCSEDIT key must first identify which system you want to make changes to by entering the server address and  RPC/Broker port number. Then, they will authenticate to the application using their access and verify codes and PIV card.
texts.text: To log in to the Data Entry for ePrescribing Controlled Substances GUI application, a user holding the XUEPCSEDIT key must first identify which system you want to make changes to by entering the server address and  RPC/Broker port number. Then, they will authenticate to the application using their access and verify codes and PIV card.

texts.orig: Launch the Data Entry for e-Prescribing Controlled Substances application.
texts.text: Launch the Data Entry for e-Prescribing Controlled Substances application.

texts.text: To select a prescriber, use these steps:

texts.text: The Look Up Utility dialog lists the providers in the system. Users can scroll through the list or use the find button to search for a name.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Another requirement of the DEA Interim Final Rule specifies that there be a “logical access control” for electronic prescribing of Controlled Substances. This is in addition to the provider having all the appropriate credentials and schedule information.
texts.text: Another requirement of the DEA Interim Final Rule specifies that there be a “logical access control” for electronic prescribing of Controlled Substances. This is in addition to the provider having all the appropriate credentials and schedule information.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With CPRS v.29, this logical access control is implemented using a new file: OE/RR EPCS PARAMETERS (#100.7). This information is stored in a new file to enable auditing, another requirement of the DEA Interim Final Rule.
texts.text: With CPRS v.29, this logical access control is implemented using a new file: OE/RR EPCS PARAMETERS (#100.7). This information is stored in a new file to enable auditing, another requirement of the DEA Interim Final Rule.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The post-install routine for OR*3.0*218 enables appropriate providers (as outlined in section 4 above). Should you need to edit a provider’s logical access control, you will need to use menu option [EPCS USER ENABLE/DISABLE].
texts.text: The post-install routine for OR*3.0*218 enables appropriate providers (as outlined in section 4 above). Should you need to edit a provider’s logical access control, you will need to use menu option [EPCS USER ENABLE/DISABLE].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Per DEA Interim Final Rule, the users with access to the GUI application for defining credentials for providers can
texts.text: Per DEA Interim Final Rule, the users with access to the GUI application for defining credentials for providers can

texts.label: text
texts.orig: be the same as the users with access to the logical access control menu option. Therefore, the menu option requires a new key: OREPCSUSERS and will not allow a holder of the XUEPCSEDIT key to use the option.
texts.text: be the same as the users with access to the logical access control menu option. Therefore, the menu option requires a new key: OREPCSUSERS and will not allow a holder of the XUEPCSEDIT key to use the option.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 11 Set up the Kernel Report device
texts.text: 11 Set up the Kernel Report device

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DEA requirements specify that certain audit reports must be generated automatically. This requires the ability to define the device. This device is defined using a parameter. Please refer to the patch description for XU*8.0*580 for instructions on setting up the parameter.
texts.text: The DEA requirements specify that certain audit reports must be generated automatically. This requires the ability to define the device. This device is defined using a parameter. Please refer to the patch description for XU*8.0*580 for instructions on setting up the parameter.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In order to meet the DEA requirements to automatically generate audit report, two Kernel reports must be scheduled in TaskMan. Please refer to the patch description for XU*8.0*580 for instructions on scheduling the two TaskMan audit reports.
texts.text: In order to meet the DEA requirements to automatically generate audit report, two Kernel reports must be scheduled in TaskMan. Please refer to the patch description for XU*8.0*580 for instructions on scheduling the two TaskMan audit reports.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Every pharmacist who will need to be able to complete controlled substance orders entered in CPRS needs to hold the PSDRPH key. It is important to remember that this key should never be assigned to a pharmacy technician.
texts.text: Every pharmacist who will need to be able to complete controlled substance orders entered in CPRS needs to hold the PSDRPH key. It is important to remember that this key should never be assigned to a pharmacy technician.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This checklist is to help sites prepare the resources and personnel they will need to install CPRS v.29. Because of the varied and complex nature of CPRS v.29’s projects, it will take some coordination among different groups to be ready to install. There are hardware tasks or checks, server installations, roles to be defined, and configuration that must occur before the CPRS v.29 installation so that the system, workstations, and providers will be ready to use to its fullest extent.
texts.text: This checklist is to help sites prepare the resources and personnel they will need to install CPRS v.29. Because of the varied and complex nature of CPRS v.29’s projects, it will take some coordination among different groups to be ready to install. There are hardware tasks or checks, server installations, roles to be defined, and configuration that must occur before the CPRS v.29 installation so that the system, workstations, and providers will be ready to use to its fullest extent.

table_cells.text:
Check that all pre-requisite patches are installed
•    XU*8.0*373
•    OR*3.0*280

Identify the following people to assist/coordinate the installation and setup:
Person responsible for OR, XU and PSD patches – only one can install the bundle, but all will need to ensure coordination
Windows Server administrator – this person will need to install the PKI Verify Server software on a Windows Server(s) and install either a patch or a cleanup script for certificates to function correctly, and must ensure that the Tumbleweed software is appropriately installed
Person with privileges to update the KERNEL SYSTEMS PARAMETERS file
Person with privileges to push out the new ePCSDataEntryfor Prescriber.exe to either the individual workstations or the shared folder
Person(s) responsible for DEA privilege configuration. The DEA regulations require at least two offices are involved. One to assign credentials such as DEA #, one to enable them for ePCS (menu option OR EPCS MENU)
Person to provide a list of pharmacists allowed to complete controlled substance orders
Person with privileges to allocate PSDRPH key
Person who can schedule two Kernel options in TaskMan
Person who can edit general parameters such as the new parameter XU EPCS REPORT DEVICE
Download CPRS29_DEA_SETUP.KID and CPRS29_DEA_KERNEL.ZIP from the ftp site.
Verify/install Tumbleweed on the server(s) that will run the PKI_Verify_Service.
Place PKIVerifyServerSetup.exe on at least one but no more than three Windows servers. Use PKIVerifyServerSetup to install and start PKI_Verify_Service as a Windows Service.
Set up Windows job to periodically restart PKI Verify Service.
Add IP Addresses for 1 to 3 machines, running PKI_Verify_Server, to the PKI Server (#53.1) field in the Kernel System Parameters (#8989.3) file

Distribute or install the ePCSDataEntryForPresciber.exe application on the workstations of those users holding the XUEPCSEDIT security key to set up providers for ordering of outpatient controlled substance prescriptions.
Set up users of the ePCSDataEntryforPrescriber tool

Set up providers for DEA e-Prescribing using:
•	ePCSDataEntryforPrescriber
•	OR EPCS MENU (done for existing providers in OR*3.0*218 post-install)

Schedule the two Kernel audit reports in TaskMan
XU EPCS Logical Access
XU EPCS PSDRPH Audit
Set up the parameter for the Report Device for the Kernel reports.

grid.text:
Check that all pre-requisite patches are installed
•    XU*8.0*373
•    OR*3.0*280

Identify the following people to assist/coordinate the installation and setup:
Person responsible for OR, XU and PSD patches – only one can install the bundle, but all will need to ensure coordination
Windows Server administrator – this person will need to install the PKI Verify Server software on a Windows Server(s) and install either a patch or a cleanup script for certificates to function correctly, and must ensure that the Tumbleweed software is appropriately installed
Person with privileges to update the KERNEL SYSTEMS PARAMETERS file
Person with privileges to push out the new ePCSDataEntryfor Prescriber.exe to either the individual workstations or the shared folder
Person(s) responsible for DEA privilege configuration. The DEA regulations require at least two offices are involved. One to assign credentials such as DEA #, one to enable them for ePCS (menu option OR EPCS MENU)
Person to provide a list of pharmacists allowed to complete controlled substance orders
Person with privileges to allocate PSDRPH key
Person who can schedule two Kernel options in TaskMan
Person who can edit general parameters such as the new parameter XU EPCS REPORT DEVICE
Download CPRS29_DEA_SETUP.KID and CPRS29_DEA_KERNEL.ZIP from the ftp site.
Verify/install Tumbleweed on the server(s) that will run the PKI_Verify_Service.
Place PKIVerifyServerSetup.exe on at least one but no more than three Windows servers. Use PKIVerifyServerSetup to install and start PKI_Verify_Service as a Windows Service.
Set up Windows job to periodically restart PKI Verify Service.
Add IP Addresses for 1 to 3 machines, running PKI_Verify_Server, to the PKI Server (#53.1) field in the Kernel System Parameters (#8989.3) file

Distribute or install the ePCSDataEntryForPresciber.exe application on the workstations of those users holding the XUEPCSEDIT security key to set up providers for ordering of outpatient controlled substance prescriptions.
Set up users of the ePCSDataEntryforPrescriber tool

Set up providers for DEA e-Prescribing using:
•	ePCSDataEntryforPrescriber
•	OR EPCS MENU (done for existing providers in OR*3.0*218 post-install)

Schedule the two Kernel audit reports in TaskMan
XU EPCS Logical Access
XU EPCS PSDRPH Audit
Set up the parameter for the Report Device for the Kernel reports.

table_cells.text: Create and update fields for turning on/off ePCS functionality for the site and individual users.
ePCS Kernel support – such as DEA and Detox number validation and user setup.
Exports the new PSDRPH key. Required for pharmacists to complete controlled substance orders.

grid.text: Create and update fields for turning on/off ePCS functionality for the site and individual users.
ePCS Kernel support – such as DEA and Detox number validation and user setup.
Exports the new PSDRPH key. Required for pharmacists to complete controlled substance orders.

table_cells.text: Installs and starts the PKI_VERIFY_SERVICE – used by Pharmacy when completing Outpatient Controlled Substance Orders

grid.text: Installs and starts the PKI_VERIFY_SERVICE – used by Pharmacy when completing Outpatient Controlled Substance Orders

table_cells.text: Every workstation used for CPRS prescribing of controlled substances has a functioning smart card reader
Every workstation used for CPRS prescribing of controlled substances has Tumbleweed Desktop Validator installed and OCSP is the #1 search protocol.
PIN Resetters have been assigned and trained at remote locations and off-hours tours of duty.
Contingency process for any downtime of electronic prescribing including supplies, if needed.
PIV cards for all outpatient controlled substance providers.
Contingency plan for PIV card failures.
Identify super users and confirm available for ePCS Implementation
Address change processes reviewed and modified, if necessary.
Windows servers - hotfix installed or script run
Tumbleweed installed on PKI Verify Service Windows Servers
PKI Verify Service installed on 1-3 servers.
Setup job to periodically restart PKI Verify Service
Setup PKI Server parameter in Kernel System Parameters
Kernel audit reports scheduled in TaskMan
